Name: Gilat Satellite Networks
Description:
Gilat Satellite Networks Ltd. (NASDAQ: GILT, TASE: GILT) is a leading global provider of satellite-based broadband communications. With over 35 years of experience, we develop and deliver deep technology solutions for satellite, ground, and new space connectivity, offering next-generation solutions and services for critical connectivity across commercial and defense applications.  We believe in the right of all people to be connected and are united in our resolution to provide communication solutions to all reaches of the world.

Together with our wholly-owned subsidiaries—Gilat Wavestream, Gilat DataPath, and Gilat Stellar Blu—we offer integrated, high-value solutions supporting multi-orbit constellations, Very High Throughput Satellites (VHTS), and Software-Defined Satellites (SDS) via our Commercial and Defense Divisions. Our comprehensive portfolio is comprised of a cloud-based platform and modems; high-performance satellite terminals; advanced Satellite On-the-Move (SOTM) antennas and ESAs; highly efficient, high-power Solid State Power Amplifiers (SSPA) and Block Upconverters (BUC) and includes integrated ground systems for commercial and defense markets, field services, network management software, and cybersecurity services.

Gilat’s products and tailored solutions support multiple applications including government and defense, IFC and mobility, broadband access, cellular backhaul, enterprise, aerospace, broadcast, and critical infrastructure clients all while meeting the most stringent service level requirements. For more information, please visit: http://www.gilat.com

Name: Iridium
Description:
In a world where global communications are increasingly essential, only one company connects everyone to the things that matter most, from pole to pole. Iridium Communications Inc. is the only mobile satellite service (MSS) company offering pole-to-pole coverage over the entire globe. The Iridium constellation of low-Earth orbiting (LEO), cross-linked satellites provides critical voice and data services for areas not served by terrestrial communication networks. Iridium's subscriber growth has been driven by increasing demand for reliable, secure, global communications. Iridium serves commercial markets through a worldwide network of hundreds of partners, and provides services to the U.S. Department of Defense, and other U.S. and international government agencies. 

The company's worldwide customer base represent a broad spectrum of industry, including maritime, aeronautical, government/defense, public safety, utilities, oil/gas, mining, forestry, heavy equipment and transportation.

Iridium has been a profitable company since 2004. Business is growing at an impressive rate with the constant acquisition of new customers and partners both domestically and abroad in some of the furthest reaches of the planet. These individuals recognize the value of a system that is global, secure and reliable.

The company is headquartered in McLean, Va. and is traded on the NASDAQ (IRDM).

Name: ALL.SPACE
Description:
ALL.SPACE isn’t just revolutionizing communication; we’re shaping the very future of defense operations, with the most advanced tactical multi-link mobility terminals—seamlessly maintaining wideband connectivity across multiple satellites in GEO/MEO/LEO orbits simultaneously for uninterrupted mission-critical operations. 

When faced with harsh and diverse operational conditions, limited connectivity, and the threat of service interference, ALL.SPACE ensures field-proven, reliable, high-speed communication for critical missions, whether on land or at sea. 

Our Hydra Terminal Range is fully integrated and easy-to-deploy, delivering enhanced mission assurance, collaboration and automation. With integrated Software Services, we provide intelligent, on-demand connectivity solutions that ensure seamless, secure, and resilient operations anywhere in the world. 

Stay connected. Stay mission-ready. 

Learn more at www.all.space.

Name: Grupo Tesacom
Description:
Tesacom is South America’s leading provider of remote telecommunications solutions, serving the government, maritime, oil & gas, agriculture, first responder, mining, transportation and military markets. From its offices in Argentina, Chile, Paraguay, Peru, Panamá and Uruguay, Tesacom offers a wide range of integrated communications solutions. Those include satellite voice and data, text messaging, email and file transfer, network integration, IoT solutions, software services, professional, consulting and field-support services – as well as Spanish, Portuguese and English 7x24 customer and network support. 
For more information visit www.tesacom.net.

Name: Kymeta Corporation
Description: Kymeta revolutionizes satellite communications through Intelligent Communications Platforms (ICPs). Founded in 2012 and headquartered in Redmond, Washington, Kymeta leverages cutting-edge metamaterial-based science to engineer, manufacture and deliver resilient connectivity and enhanced situational awareness for critical, mobile applications.  Backed by U.S. and international patents our electronically steered flat panel antennas enable seamless communications on-the-move. With software-defined solutions, hybrid multi-network capabilities, and edge processing, we integrate satellite and cellular networks, ensuring continuous connectivity in challenging environments. Kymeta solutions serve government, military, maritime, transport, and public safety clients worldwide, providing uninterrupted connectivity and spatial intelligence anywhere, anytime. With over 150 patents, Kymeta is shaping the future of global communications, dedicated to delivering reliable, efficient, and intelligent connectivity solutions for an increasingly mobile and connected world.

Name: Leaf Space
Description:
Maximize connectivity to your satellite with our cost-effective, global ground station network. Our automated scheduling service ensures optimal contact time, tailored to your mission's needs. 
Trusted by over 55 clients in 100+ missions, we offer:

 Unlimited Ground Station Access: Access one or all stations at a fixed price per minute.

 High Compatibility: Support for many COTS radios as we continue working with the customer to onboard new ones.

 Global Network: 20 antennas across 15 strategic locations, constantly evolving with the needs of our customers.

 Easy-to-use API: Our RESTful API is already accessible with well-established commercial Mission Control Software and is easy to integrate.

 Licensing: We absorb the costs of ground-segment licensing and obtain licenses with zero hassle for the customer.

Ready to Simplify Your Satellite Connectivity? Whether you're in the midst of an active mission or the early stages of planning, it's never too early or too late to explore how Leaf Space can streamline your operations.
 
Contact us to learn more about how Leaf Space can help your mission.

Name: Satcom Direct
Description:
Gogo is the only multi-orbit, multi-band in-flight connectivity provider offering connectivity technology purpose-built for business and military/government aviation. Its industry-leading product portfolio offers best-in-class solutions for all aircraft types, from small to large and heavy jets and beyond. 

The Gogo offering uniquely incorporates Air-to-Ground systems with high-speed satellite networks, to deliver consistent, global tip-to-tail connectivity through a sophisticated suite of software, hardware, and advanced infrastructure supported by a 24/7/365 in person customer support team. 

Gogo consistently strives to set new standards for reliability, security and innovation and is shaping the future of inflight aviation to make it easier for every customer to stay connected beyond all expectations.

Name: TESAT
Description:
𝗪𝗲 𝗱𝗲𝗹𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗿 𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘀𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝘁𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝘂𝗻𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀. 𝗙𝗼𝗿 𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘆𝗼𝗻𝗲. 𝗙𝗼𝗿 𝗮 𝗯𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱.

Data transmission via satellite is indispensable for sustainable development. We supply the key components for satellite communications and are continuously improving them. As the leading manufacturer of secure space-based communications, we are helping to connect the world and make it a better place to live. 

Every day, we are working hard on our contribution to sustainable development: 
We help people to connect (telephony), stay informed (satellite TV, internet), and find their way (navigation).

We enable better earth observation for climate, disaster prevention, security and higher yields in agriculture.

We contribute to scientific progress. We cooperate with universities and serve various scientific projects such as the James Webb telescope.

On our 63,000m² premises in Backnang, Germany, about 1,100 employees develop, assemble, integrate, and test products, systems and solutions for satellite communications. To date, nearly 1,000 space projects have been completed. More than half of all communication satellites in orbit have TESAT equipment on board.

We offer the complete communication technology necessary, for example to emit television signals via satellite antennas to each household. As the first organization in the world, we have been developing and delivering equipment for optical broadband communication in space. More than 85,000 optical links have been successfully performed so far.

From a social point of view, we value different perspectives, backgrounds, experiences, skills and individual qualities. Together, we stand for a culture of open discussion, integrity, focus, entrepreneurial thinking and action, and leadership by example.

Environmental protection and the economical use of resources are part of our daily activities, from product development to production.

Name: Satlink
Description:
Founded in 1992, Satlink is a world leading technology provider that develops solutions focused on ocean knowledge and sustainability, improving the management of ocean resources and working closely with industry, governments and NGOs. Strongly committed to fisheries sustainability and ocean conservation, Satlink's mission is to provide tools that can help fleets and governments worldwide to sustainably manage their activity in the most efficient way. 

Headquartered in Madrid, Satlink has offices in Spain (Marin), Canada, Seychelles, Ecuador, USA, Fiji, Solomon Islands, Korea, and Taiwan while working with local partners in other locations.

Name: Sama X
Description:
At Sama X, an authorized Starlink reseller, we are accelerating access to digital prosperity by delivering turnkey satellite internet solutions wherever they’re needed most—no matter how remote, rugged, or infrastructure-challenged.

Headquartered in Dubai, we operate across the Middle East, Africa, and South Asia, connecting businesses, governments, and communities through secure, high-speed, and low-latency internet powered by Starlink’s cutting-edge LEO technology.

Driven by a customer-first mindset and deep local expertise, Sama X empowers key sectors including Oil & Gas, Construction, Telecom, and Public Services. Whether enabling field operations, supporting emergency response, or driving digital transformation, we make connectivity simple, scalable, and reliable.

Rooted in trust, performance, and partnership, our mission is to simplify access to digital connectivity and unlock new possibilities for progress—wherever your business takes you.

Name: Space Norway
Description:
Space Norway is Northern Europe’s leading satellite operator and a key player in the European space industry. 

We provide advanced and reliable satellite connectivity services to governments, public and defence sectors, commercial maritime, land-based industries, and broadcasters. Our portfolio of satellites, teleport infrastructure and fibre optic subsea cables enables us to deliver robust solutions that meet the needs of clients in Europe, the Middle East, North Africa, the Arctic and Antarctica.

Our team’s industry-leading expertise, agility and commitment to excellence allow us to respond swiftly to market demands, accelerating technological breakthroughs.

Our mission is to harness space for security and commercial growth - enriching life and expanding possibilities.

Space Norway is fully-owned by the Norwegian Ministry of Trade, Industry and Fisheries. Space Norway holds a 50% ownership in Kongsberg Satellite Services (KSAT).

Space Norway. Space for More.

Name: Goonhilly
Description:
Goonhilly enables the safe, sustainable, and secure use of space – keeping organisations connected and informed, from Earth to the Moon and beyond.

We offer services across three customer-focused divisions: Lunar and Deep Space Communications, National Security, and Data Centre Services.

Visit our sister company COMSAT to learn more about our Commercial Satcom services across our international teleports.

Name: Global Satcoms
Description:
Global Satcoms stands as a prominent global field service engineering firm, specializing in VSAT technologies. Our core competency revolves around catering to VSAT service providers and teleport operators, primarily emphasizing the oil & gas and maritime sectors.

Operating robustly in numerous countries globally, we deliver dependable and effective satellite communication solutions to our esteemed clientele. Our skilled professionals handle everything from installation and maintenance to troubleshooting, ensuring our clients experience seamless connectivity and uninterrupted operations.

Trust Global Satcoms for all your VSAT needs, as we deliver exceptional service wherever you are in the world.

Name: Northtelecom
Description:
Northtelecom headquartered in Dubai, UAE specializes in providing satellite communication / ICT services and solutions. Northtelecom’s service portfolio offer innovative and reliable communication solutions to the industries like Energy, Maritime, Media, Enterprise, Telco, & Humanitarian.

Established in the year 2007 and with 18 years of rich industry experience, Northtelecom has succeeded in delivering comprehensive package of system integration, system products, and network communication services enabling a complete end-to-end solution to its customers.
Northtelecom has its foot prints spread globally with offices in UAE, Germany, Malaysia, Italy and Singapore and teleports Frankfurt, Kuala Lumpur, Singapore & Athens.
www.northtelecom.com

Name: AsiaSat
Description: Asia Satellite Telecommunications Co. Ltd. (AsiaSat) offers reliable satellite connectivity, media and data solutions to customers in the broadcast, telecom and mobility sectors through its fleet of six in-orbit satellites - AsiaSat 4, AsiaSat 5, AsiaSat 6, AsiaSat 7, AsiaSat 8 and AsiaSat 9, and teleport infrastructure. From content distribution to headends, telcos, DTH, DTT platforms; Occasional Use; to One Click Go Live video streaming; IP-based, hybrid OTT service; hosting service; cutting edge VSAT solutions serving aviation, maritime, mobile backhaul, AsiaSat helps bridge the digital divide, aiming to be the foremost satellite solutions provider, an instinctive partner of choice in the Asia-Pacific. For more information, please visit www.asiasat.com

Name: Falcon Mega Solutions - Vessel Tracking & Satellite Communication
Description:
About Us

Falcon Mega Solutions (FMS) is a leading service provider, and offers a state-of-the-art Vessel Tracking, Monitoring, Ship Security Alert Solution and Satellite Communications services to Offshore, Marine and Fishing Industry.
 
We develop Satellite-based Innovative and Reliable Solution that enable our customers to monitor and manage their vessels worldwide seamlessly.
 
Inmarsat satellite based Type Approve Hardware provides dependable communication for remote Management of Assets in the Transportation, Maritime, Oil & Gas, Heavy Equipment Sectors.

Our Vision
We understands that business value cannot be achieved through technology alone. It starts with people: experts working together to get to the heart of your individual business objectives and develop the most adapted solutions to fit these requirements.
 
We have a strong commitment for the continuous Research & Development and Improvement of our Solutions and Services. We aim to be the dominant Service Provider in the Maritime Security, Asset Monitoring, Tracking and Surveillance worldwide.

Our Mission - “Safe & Secure Sea”
Our mission is to provide reliable and efficient technology to assist in making the Safe & Secure Sea for the vessels and their crew.
 
Technology plays a vital role to achieve the mission. Our Remote Fleet Monitoring and Ship Security Alert Systems are based on the Sophisticated and Reliable Satellite Technology to provide seamless Services beyond the limitations.

#marine #trackingsolutions #monitoring #securitysolutions 
#vesseltracking #shipsecurityalertsystem #marineassettracking 
#falconmegasolutions #fms #vesseltrackingandsatellitecommunication 
#falconmegatrack #offshore #FishingIndustry#boattracking #shiptracking #maritime #fleetmanagement #ShipManagement #marineengineering

Website:
www . falconmegasolutions . com

Name: Astrolight
Description: Astrolight is on a mission to bridge the connectivity gap between space and Earth with advanced laser communication systems. Founded in 2019, the company aims to provide end-to-end connectivity solutions by building complete and self-contained hardware for ground and space operational domains. Astrolight produces energy-efficient optical technologies for LEO-to-Earth links, ship-to-ship communication, and ground-based systems in secure defense and commercial applications.

Name: Startical
Description: Startical aspires to revolutionize Air Traffic Management (ATM) with the launch of a satellite constellation. This network will provide surveillance (ADS-B) and communication (VHF) services in remote and oceanic regions that terrestrial infrastructure can’t reach. The service aims at enhancing flight safety, increasing capacity and efficiency, boosting punctuality, and cutting global CO2 emissions.

Name: Ovzon
Description:
CONNECTING THE WORLD’S CRITICAL MISSIONS VIA SATELLITE
Ovzon offers world-leading integrated mobile satellite communications services, SATCOM-as-a-Service, to customers globally. The services combine high-throughput satellite networks, mobile satellite terminals, gateway services, and dedicated customer support. Ovzon's offerings meet the growing demand for mission-critical connectivity for customers with high performance, mobility, and resiliency requirements such as Defense, National Security, and Public Safety. On July 5, 2024, commercial service commenced on Ovzon’s proprietary developed geostationary satellite Ovzon 3. Ovzon has offices in Stockholm, Sweden, Herndon, VA, and Tampa, FL, USA. Ovzon is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm Small Cap.

Name: Apogeo Space
Description: Apogeo Space is a newspace company which aims to build a constellation of picosatellites capable of providing Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity to everyone, everywhere.

Name: hiSky
Description:
hiSky is the leader of agile IoT networks, enabling businesses, people and machines the freedom to connect from any device, anywhere, always.

We provide a reliable and affordable IoT and hybrid network with an easy-to-use cloud-based management platform that includes monitoring, alerting and billing capabilities.

Our agile IoT networks ensure full redundancy with swift transition between network layers, turning any end-user device into a hybrid-ready one.

Companies benefit from our proven connectivity to cover every terrain, establish private commercial IoT networks, gain insights from high volume secure data and more.

Name: EchoStar Mobile
Description:
EchoStar Mobile has designed a single pan-European S-band LoRa IoT network, providing bi-directional, real-time connectivity to LoRa sensors across the coverage with no additional infrastructure required. Our seamless coverage and LoRa network design ensure that users across Europe, the UK, and Scandinavia do not need cumbersome roaming agreements to achieve ubiquitous service continuity across geographic regions, making it ideal for mobile customers or those customers with widely distributed sites.

Our dual-mode terrestrial and satellite module design means devices integrating with our LoRa module can connect to local terrestrial networks if required. As a member of the LoRa Alliance, we are focused on ensuring our LoRa module works seamlessly with the existing ecosystem, making it easy to integrate our network into existing projects and making the ideal technology choice for deployments where coverage and infrastructure make adopting LoRa problematic.

Name: Gatehouse Satcom
Description:
For more than two decades we have delivered market-leading communications software products to the satellite communications industry, and we are looking forward to continuing this role in the new era of standardized and direct-to-device satellite connectivity.

We offer certified satellite communication software and network test solutions for software, hardware, and system integration.

Are you looking for a development partner for your satellite communication project? We provide a range of satcom software products and development services – from complete solutions to protocol stacks, frameworks and components.

See cases and get the latest insight from Gatehouse Satcom right here: https://gatehousesatcom.com/insights/

Name: Wyld Networks
Description:
Wyld Networks enables low power, low-cost global connectivity for the Internet of Things via low earth orbit satellites using LoRa protocol. 

Founded in 2016, Wyld's hybrid solution enables IoT objects to seamlessly connect to both existing terrestrial LPWAN networks and LEO satellites.

Wyld Networks is listed on the NASDAQ FN exchange.

Name: Advantech Wireless Inc. as of 2018 is now Advantech Wireless Technologies Inc.
Description:
For current information, please visit the Advantech Wireless Technologies Inc. LinkedIn page or the company website @ www.advantechwireless.com
https://www.linkedin.com/search/results/all/?fetchDeterministicClustersOnly=true&heroEntityKey=urn%3Ali%3Aorganization%3A10140608&keywords=advantech%20wireless%20technologies&origin=RICH_QUERY_SUGGESTION&position=0&searchId=261f7ff9-956c-4e89-a174-b591395ec7a3&sid=~(n&spellCorrectionEnabled=false

Name: 6G-NTN
Description: The 6G-NTN project seeks to achieve complete and seamless integration of Non-Terrestrial Network (NTN) components into the 6G system by researching and developing innovative technical, commercial, regulatory, and standardization enablers. 6G-NTN’s vision is to extend the next-generation mobile networks' resilience, sustainability, and coverage while meeting consumer and vertical market segment needs and expectations, launching new value chains, and having a significant social impact.

Name: UNIO Enterprise
Description:
As vehicles transform into tech-savvy hubs resembling smartphones on wheels, the demand for always-on connectivity is bigger than ever. Currently, the inconsistent network coverage is impacting efficiency, safety, and overall user experience in connected vehicles. 

UNIO bridges 5G and satellite networks for all mobility use cases, powering safe, seamless and always-on connectivity for services, vehicles and applications across the globe. 

As a joint venture of IABG, Isar Aerospace, Reflex Aerospace, Mynaric, OHB SE and SES, UNIO is the only European company combining the expertise of leading space players from all core areas crucial to providing all-in-one connectivity solutions.

Name: Station Satcom
Description:
Founded in 1998, Station Satcom is a global leader in integrated remote communication solutions. With over 25 years of expertise, we deliver end-to-end services across Satellite Communications, Navigation Communication, Vessel IT, Cybersecurity, and IOT Solutions designed to perform in the world’s most demanding environments.

With offices in over 9+ countries and strategic partnerships in more than 21 regions, our solutions power operations across land, sea, and air. From commercial vessels to critical infrastructure, we support industries including maritime, defense, offshore energy, logistics, aviation, and enterprise.

We are trusted for our ability to deliver reliable, secure, and high-performance connectivity, backed by a global support network and a commitment to innovation. Whether enabling vessel tracking, crew welfare, onboard surveillance, or cyber protection, we ensure seamless communication and operational uptime—wherever you are.

At Station Satcom, connectivity isn't just what we do—it's how we empower the world to operate smarter, safer, and stronger.

Name: NAL Technologies
Description: NAL Technologies (Naltec) is a leader in advanced connectivity and critical assurance technology. We deliver cutting-edge SATCOM and positioning, navigational, and timing (PNT) solutions to meet evolving government, defense, and commercial needs. Our powerful military-grade devices, innovative hardware, and flexible cloud-based applications ensure continuous connectivity and operational performance from any location in the world.

Name: Blue Sky Network
Description:
Blue Sky Network is an industry-leading provider of global solutions engineered for the future of land, sea, and air communications. 

Founded in 2001, our deep expertise in connectivity and intelligent fleet management, combined with a collaborative, solutions-first approach toward innovation, enables customers to reliably track, manage, and engage their high-value assets in real time from any location in the world.

Headquartered in Scottsdale, Arizona, with an office in São Paulo, Brazil, we serve a customer base and partner network spanning more than 50 countries.

Follow us for company and solutions updates, career opportunities, and more.

Name: WARPSPACE
Description:
With a DNA structured on innovation and a heritage whose origins can be traced back to JAXA and OICETS-based projects, as well as supply chain innovations in NewSpace, WARPSPACE aims to become the world's first private company to provide a globally accessible optical communication network in space.

Every year the number of spacecraft launched continues to increase, the demand for communications connectivity grows exponentially, and the existing infrastructure for conventional communications is no longer sustainable. The World needs a solution. The World needs WARPSPACE.

Based in Japan, WARPSPACE, a privately held, space-tech start-up company, is shaping the future of space communications with WarpHub InterSat, the World's first optical inter-satellite data relay network service. WarpHub InterSat provides a cost-effective, high-speed optical link with persistent connectivity for LEO satellites and enables uninterrupted space-to-ground communication in near real-time.

WarpHub InterSat is an ideal solution for any satellite operator, private or public, government or military, with an Earth observation mission and the need to move large volumes of data from space to the ground on demand.

Name: Regional African Satellite Communications Organization
Global Invacom Group
Description: Global Invacom Group designs, develops and manufactures ground equipment for Satellite Communications (SatCom), Broadcast (SatTV)t, and Global Navigation (GNSS) networks. Serving GEO, MEO and LEO constellations, our technology, products and solution are deployed in over 120 countries serving the world's largest satellite operators.

Name: AccelerComm Ltd
Description:
AccelerComm is a leading provider of high-performance, error-resilient physical layer solutions for 5G Non Terrestrial Networks (NTN). Experts in specialist Radio Access Networks (RAN) applications, AccelerComm delivers solutions with cutting-edge performance and power efficiency, utilising next generation compute platforms. 

The company’s comprehensive portfolio ranges from customisable signal processing components through to complete physical layer solutions, implemented over a wide range of platforms, including software, AI Engines, FPGA, and ASIC-ready IP cores. Its modular approach allows seamless integration based on O-RAN, SCF and 3GPP standards. 

AccelerComm’s solutions can be configured for a wide range of applications, optimizing aspects such as power consumption, latency, reliability, and resilience. Its technology addresses the unique challenges of NTN satellite communications, offering space-hardened, field-proven implementations that meet stringent SWaP (Size, Weight and Power) requirements. 

Through continuous innovation, AccelerComm ensures its customers have access to the latest advancements in physical layer technology, supporting the development of tomorrow’s high-performance, low-power networks.

Name: Santander Teleport
Description:
Santander Teleport is an independent teleport operator offering satellite communication services in C, X, Ku and Ka bands for service providers, satellite operators, enterprise and government organisations in a number of markets including maritime, enterprise, telcos, broadcast and defence.

Santander Teleport owns its own satellite teleport facilities in Spain, has access to a global terrestrial network and works with partner teleports around the world to provide global reach.

Santander Teleport provides the following services:

MSS and VSAT Satellite communication services
Uplink/downlink/turnaround 
Antenna hosting and Colocation of customer equipment
Custom VSAT solutions
Connectivity to major PoPs and end customer sites
Back-up / Disaster recovery
Project Engineering and Consultancy
Specialised services for satellite operators (IOT, TT&C, RCM...)

Name: Sternula
Description:
Sternula is world's first commercial provider of satellite-based AIS 2.0, also known as VHF Data Exchange System (VDES). AIS 2.0 (VDES) is designed to enable communication between the ship bridge and shore.

We are building our own global satellite infrastructure which is co-exist with VDES coastal radio for near-share use cases. The first satellite is operational from early 2023.

For more information visit our website.

Name: Requtech - SATCOM technologies
Description:
Requtech is a SATCOM product developer based in Linköping, Sweden. We deliver fully integrated terminal solutions for LEO, MEO, GEO, and Troposcatter connectivity. We offer robust and reliable high-performance SATCOM terminals based on cutting-edge technology.

Our product range:
ESA terminals: The RESA series 
Reflector terminals: The PICO Series 
Troposcatter terminals: The TROPOLINE series

Name: Orbyt Global Group
Description:
At Orbyt Global Group, we empower the maritime industry through next-generation technology solutions that deliver smarter control, operational efficiency, and always-on connectivity. 
With a global presence and headquarters in Piraeus, Greece, we support shipping companies worldwide in navigating the digital era with confidence and clarity.
Our strength lies in our technology superiority, personalized support, and commitment to delivering an exceptional customer experience - anytime, anywhere.
What We Do:
 Hybrid Airtime Connectivity
Seamless, global communication through a smart blend of satellite (Ka, Ku, L, and C bands) and terrestrial 4G/5G networks.
 E-Navigation Solutions 
Smarter route planning, real-time fleet tracking, digital charts, and live meteorological data - all in one ecosystem.
 Digital Bridge Applications
Fully digitized bridge systems with IoT-enabled smart applications that unlock real-time, data-driven decision-making.
 Total Bridge Solutions
Supplying, maintaining, and modernizing all bridge electronics for maximum performance and compliance.
 ORBYT Nexus Platform
Our modular tech platform, built to transform maritime operations with AI-driven automation, real-time intelligence, and enterprise-grade cybersecurity - ensuring scalability, resilience, and peace of mind.
We proudly serve 750+ clients and 6,000+ vessels across the globe, helping improve vessel performance, crew welfare, and decision-making through intelligent, connected solutions.
At Orbyt Global, we don’t just connect ships - we unlock the full potential of maritime intelligence.

Name: Mobile Satellite Services Association (MSSA)
Description: The Mobile Satellite Services Association (MSSA) advances the development of direct-to-device (D2D) and IoT connectivity through an ecosystem of Non-Terrestrial Network (NTN) providers that are committed to seamlessly extending mobile coverage globally. Founding members Viasat, Terrestar Solutions, Ligado Networks, Omnispace and Yahsat support a vision focused on facilitating the integration and evolution of terrestrial and satellite mobile networks to deliver scalable, sustainable, and affordable high-performance cellular-like services to any device, anytime, anywhere in the world. To support this rapidly emerging market, the non-profit association will work to ensure MSS L- and S-band operators play a central role in facilitating the future of a robust and competitive D2D services market. Through the coordinated deployment of technical standards and enhancement of regulatory frameworks, the MSSA drives new initiatives to foster support for MSS-based services leveraging the 3GPP mobile standards. For more information, visit www.mss-association.org.

Name: SKY Perfect JSAT (スカパーＪＳＡＴ)
Description:
We are SKY Perfect JSAT, a Tokyo-based satellite operator with over 35 years of experience in providing reliable satellite communications and broadcasting solutions. Our fleet of 17 geostationary satellites offer coverage spanning from North America to the Indian Ocean Region.

In our Space Business, we provide a wide range of satellite services across land, sea and air, playing a vital role in supporting both commercial & governmental connectivity needs. We are expanding the Space Intelligence Business by leveraging satellite data and developing the non-terrestrial network “Universal NTN,” which will utilize GEO, non-GEO, and HAPS to enhance connectivity, improve disaster preparedness, and contribute to national security, and support the realization of a super-smart society. 

In our Media Business, we operate Japan’s “SKY PerfecTV!” Multi-channel Pay-TV platform and extend services through our Fiber-optic Alliance Business and tailored Media Solutions. We are also exploring Connected TV and Web3-related operations to drive growth in the evolving media landscape.  

Our regional offices are located in Singapore and Jakarta. In North America, we provide satellite services through our U.S. subsidiary JSAT International Inc., based in Washington, D.C. 


スカパーＪＳＡＴは、宇宙事業とメディア事業を両輪とする国内唯一の『宇宙実業社』です。

宇宙事業ではアジア最大の衛星通信事業者として、「スカパー!」伝送に加え、航空機向けインターネット回線や災害時バックアップ回線等を提供しています。35年にわたり宇宙ビジネスを営み、培ってきた知見・実績を活かし、事業領域を拡大しながら、防災・安全保障分野に留まらず、安心で快適な社会の実現に貢献します。

メディア事業では、国内唯一のCSデジタル放送を運営するプラットフォーム事業者として、有料多チャンネル放送「スカパー!」をはじめとした放送・配信事業を提供しています。光回線を経由した地上波・BS/CS再送信サービスを提供する光アライアンス事業など収益源の多角化にも取り組み、多様で創造性豊かな社会の実現に貢献します。

Name: Alp Communication
Description:
Alp Communication technologies founded in 2024 by engineers with deep expertise and an innovative vision. India Space Congress 2024 (ISC-2024) 'Space Innovation: Bridging Boundaries, Transforming Tomorrow' is a strategic assemblage of national and international space industry leaders offering primary access to opportunities in the rapidly growing sector in India for the worldwide space ecosystem.

The foundation of the India Space Congress [ISC] is a multi-stream program highlighting the evolution of the Indian space economy and opportunities for its development and growth.

Hosted for the third time by the SatCom Industry Association, [SIA-India], ISC 2024 is aglobal congregation of industry experts, government, policymakers, thought leaders, legal professionals and academia in the space sector to discuss strategic measures to efficiently manage space exploration, new disruptive space-based services and applications, viable business models, space situational awareness and cyber security.
The vision is to bring all the high-level stakeholders together from space agencies, industry, and institutions around the world under one roof to swap insights, strategies, and rising trends for collaborative development of the space ecosystem in India and bring global and regional economic benefits.

Name: Abiental Geospace
Description:
A ABIENTAL é uma plataforma ambiental de última geração que transforma a forma como dados da natureza são registrados, validados e protegidos. Desenvolvida no Brasil, conecta pesquisadores, equipes e instituições com precisão, rastreabilidade e inteligência artificial. Permite coletas ambientais mesmo offline, com geolocalização automática, identificação de espécies por foto, denúncias georreferenciadas e sincronização em tempo real. Cada dado coletado é validado com identificador único (ULID) e armazenado na nuvem, garantindo histórico auditável e confiável.

Com presença em dispositivos móveis, tablets e web, a ABIENTAL oferece um ecossistema colaborativo com funcionalidades como mapa interativo, modo noturno tático, criação de projetos, relatórios técnicos completos e suporte multilíngue. Tudo pensado para facilitar auditorias, certificações e tomadas de decisão em campo.

Em parceria com a Trust Carbon, a ABIENTAL vai além e possibilita que propriedades rurais a partir de 1 hectare sejam analisadas e certificadas para o mercado de créditos de carbono. A Trust Carbon utiliza análise via satélite com IA, validação por vídeo com coordenadas GPS e emissão de certificado digital global em até 48h. Todo o processo é seguro, rastreável e baseado em blockchain, garantindo transparência e exclusividade no mapa global de áreas certificadas.

Juntas, ABIENTAL e Trust Carbon democratizam o acesso à proteção ambiental e ao mercado de carbono — unindo tecnologia, ciência e impacto real para quem vive e cuida da natureza.

Сегодня FleetMonitoring - это:
передовое оборудование европейского качества
собственный центр it разработок
Не имеющее аналогов ПО и мобильное приложение
более 15 лет опыта в сфере транспортной телематике
более 3200 клиентов по всему миру

Наши решения помогают транспортно-логистическим компаниям оптимизировать управление автопарком, снижать расходы и повышать эффективность.
 Мы внедряем системы мониторинга транспорта и расхода топлива, повышаем эффективность использования подвижного состава за счет инструментов экономичного вождени (EcoDriving), а также помогаем реализовать интеграции с партнерскими решениями. Наши инструменты способствуют снижению аварийности и минимизации экологического воздействия, делая транспортные процессы более безопасными и устойчивыми.

Name: Telecom Infrastructure Partners Estonia
Description:
Kujutage ette järgmist olukorda: viis, kümme või isegi viisteist aastat tagasi sõlmisite telekommunikatsiooniettevõttega rendilepingu oma kinnistule tugijaama rajamiseks. Sellest hetkest alates alustasite koostööd ja regulaarne renditasu toetas Teie eelarvet. Mõne aja pärast hakkasid Teil tekkima kahtlused:

 Kas lepingu tingimused on ajaga muutunud Teie jaoks ebasoodsamaks, mis omakorda vähendab sissetulekut?
 Kas operaatoril on õigus leping ennetähtaegselt lõpetada ilma Teie süüta?
 Kas rendisumma on praegu kehtivatele turuhindadele vastav?
 Kas Teil on piisav positsioon, et alustada suure ettevõttega läbirääkimisi tingimuste ajakohasemateks muutmiseks?
 
Meie mõistame Teie kahtlusi, sest enamikul juhtudel on need põhjendatud. Meie ettevõte on aastaid lahendanud sarnaseid dilemmasid, investeerides märkimisväärseid summasid aktiivse rendilepinguga kinnistutesse ning ettevõtetesse. Selle kõrvalt ka toetame ja nõustame oma potentsiaalseid kliente telekommunikatsiooniettevõtetega läbirääkimistel.
 
Siin on mõned olulised detailid selle kohta, kes Me oleme ja mida Me teeme:

 Oleme rahvusvaheline ettevõte, kes tegutseb telekommunikatsioonitööstuses juba üle 2 aasta.
 Me tegutseme üle maailma rohkem kui 16 riigis, kus oleme juba teinud mitmeid tuhandeid investeeringuid.
 Poolas teeme koostööd juba üle saja era-ja avaliku sektori ettevõttega. 
 Ühekordsed maksed ulatuvad kuni 3 500 000 EUR-ni.
 Meie poolt makstud vahendeid ei tagastata isegi juhul kui renditasu väheneb, lepingut ei pikendata või kui operaator lõpetab selle ennetähtaegselt. Kogu risk jääb Meie kanda.
 
Kui Meie uskumiseks ainult sõnadest ei piisa, vaadake ja kuulake mida on Meie kliendid õelnud - https://youtube.com/@TelecomInfrastructurePartners

Name: PT JARINGAN TELEKOMUNIKASI INDONESIA
Description:
Jumlah pelanggan telepon seluler di Indonesia mencapai 355,62 juta pelanggan pada 2020. Angka tersebut meningkat 4,20% dibanding tahun 2019, bahkan melebihi jumlah penduduk Indonesia pada tahun 2020 yang sebanyak 270 juta jiwa. Tantangan bagi operator adalah bagaimana mengelola pertumbuhan sambil mengurangi biaya dan merebut pangsa pasar. Karena operator telekomunikasi seluler berfokus pada pembuatan, penerapan dan penjualan layanan seluler, maka harus dibangun infrastruktur jaringan yang menyediakan kapasitas, ketersediaan, dan kinerja kompetitif. Laju perubahan mendorong operator untuk mencari metode terbaik untuk mengurangi kompleksitas, terutama di lingkungan multi-vendor.
PT Jaringan Telekomunikasi Indonesia atau JARTEL merupakan badan usaha yang berfokus pada penyediaan layanan untuk jaringan telekomunikasi seluler, modul kinerja dan implementasi, pengiriman dan perbaikan (SPMS).  JARTEL sebagai unit bisnis independent terus mengembangkan kualitas dalam memberikan pelayanan terhadap mitra bisnis. 
JARTEL sebagai Badan Usaha Pelaksana telah berhasil membangun jaringan microwave dan Base Transceiver Station (BTS) yang bekerjasama dengan vendor perangkat seperti NOKIA, ZTE dan Huawei untuk wilayah Indonesia meliputi Jawa Barat, Jawa Tengah dan Jawa Timur.  JARTEL memberikan kualitas layanan yang tinggi kepada mitra bisnis dengan memastikan kinerja jaringan seluler dan dapat mengidentifikasi setiap masalah terkait kinerja pada server jaringan dan lingkungan penyediaan layanan.
Kedalaman keterlibatan JARTEL disesuaikan kasus per kasus, dari konsultasi strategi teknologi sampai dengan pelaksanaan proyek yang optimal sesuai target dari operator dalam pelaksanaan kerjasama penjaminan kualitas layanan jangka panjang.

Name: SpaceTech Malaysia Association
Description:
The SpaceTech Malaysia Association (SMA) is the country’s first industry-led body dedicated to advancing Malaysia’s position in the global space economy. Formed to catalyze collaboration across government, industry, academia, and international partners, SMA supports national objectives outlined in the Dasar Angkasa Negara 2030 and Malaysia Space Exploration 2030 (MSE2030).

SMA serves as the unified voice of Malaysia’s space industry—driving policy alignment, investment facilitation, talent development, and innovation in satellite-based services. Our mission is to transform space from a frontier of exploration into a platform for socio-economic development, digital sovereignty, and national resilience.

With a strong focus on equatorial nations, SMA leads Malaysia’s AI-Infused Satellite-as-a-Service (AI-SaaS) strategy—developing and delivering satellite-powered, AI-enhanced solutions in areas such as agriculture, disaster management, urban development, maritime security, and environmental monitoring.

We are also convening the Equatorial AI-Sat Initiative (EASI)—a South-South cooperation framework enabling near-equatorial countries to co-develop, share, and benefit from satellite infrastructure and data. Through this, Malaysia is positioning itself as a strategic hub for AI-powered space services across ASEAN, Africa, and Latin America.

Key Focus Areas:

1) National satellite infrastructure and data sovereignty
2) AI-powered space applications for socio-economic impact
3) Space policy and regulatory advancement
4) Industry investment and market facilitation
5) Global partnerships and regional diplomacy
6) STEAM education and workforce development

SMA is committed to turning strategic vision into reality—bridging the gap between policy, technology, and real-world application to ensure space contributes meaningfully to national growth, digital inclusion, and regional cooperation.

For more information or membership inquiries, visit www.spacetech-malaysia.my

Buyers and sellers confidentially state their need to us and SpectraNet will identify possible counterparties and support the transaction. Spectrum suppliers can make more efficient use of their valuable spectrum assets through leasing them to other satellite operators in need. This leasing represents a lucrative passive income stream while also contributing to broader space sector development.

We are pleased to announce that SpectraNet has been declared the winner of the 2023 EuroSpaceHub New Space and Entrepreneurship competition. SpectraNet's core value is the more flexible utilisation of space RF spectrum and therefore increased value to humanity from future space endeavours.

ITSNT 2019 and ITSNT 2020 were skipped due to re-organizations and sanitary issues.

This event is composed of “Invited Guest” sessions where special guests are invited to give a presentation related to the generic topic of the session. Typically, there are 4 guest speakers per session, and the session ends with a round table.
When held physically, the ITSNT also provides a great environment for networking and visiting our sponsors’ exhibition stands.
The symposium includes tutorials given by some of the invited guest speakers on their topic of excellence.
The 2023 edition of the ITSNT will take place on the 29 and 30 of June 2023

Core Services:
• RF Test & Measurement (G/T, EIRP, Cross-Pol, Beamwidth, Isolation, PIM, VSWR, Group Delay, Phase Noise)
• Antenna Control Systems (Vertex, CPI, Radeus Labs) – Integration, Optimization, Troubleshooting, Retrofits
• Mechanical & Optical Alignments (Theodolite alignment, pedestal verticality, encoder reference, subreflector optimization)
• Site Implementation & Customer Representation (FAT/SAT, field operations management, preventive maintenance)

Why Parity Global:
• Principal Engineer with 25+ years of SATCOM expertise
• Deployments in 50+ countries – global field reputation
• Trusted by primes, OEMs, and government clients
• Active DoD Secret Clearance | CAGE: 70US5 | DUNS: 021559411 | UEI: S9R9KL71ZCK5

Name: GapSat Development Group Ltd.
Description:
Interim satellite solutions for GEO satellite operators

GapSat provides complete in-orbit geostationary communications satellites on an interim basis (usually up to three years) to satellite operators in their time of need - prior to the introduction of a permanent, dedicated satellite:
After a launch failure or an in-orbit failure;
After capacity on an operator’s location is sold out; 
Starting service on a new orbital location without having to risk the capital expenditure for a new long-term mission; and
Protecting (bringing into use – “BIU”) an ITU filing

GapSat will propose an interim satellite plan and implement the relocation manoeuvre, to bring the satellite to the customer’s orbital location.
During the contract, GapSat will retain control over the satellite, and will then move it away at the end of the specified period.

Name: FreeFall Aerospace
Description:
FreeFall Aerospace is developing low-cost antenna technology for ground and space. These lightweight, low-power antennas are ideally suited to support the new LEO/MEO satellites for continuous global communications with efficient beam steering and consistent high gain at a fraction of the cost of existing solutions. FreeFall develops space and ground systems using spherical reflector technology from radio astronomy and planetary exploration research. This expertise provides a new perspective on the challenges of high-frequency satellite communication. 

FreeFall's technology also has the potential to revolutionize communication for small UAVs and other aerial platforms for efficient Over-The-Horizon command, control, and communication. Various commercial and government organizations recognize FreeFall's approach as potentially disruptive and of high value. 

Located in Tucson, Arizona, FreeFall maintains a close working relationship with the University of Arizona, which serves as a continuous source of innovative concepts and a "force multiplier" for technology and product development.

FreeFall Aerospace earned the 2019 Startup Innovator of the Year Award from the Arizona Commerce Authority and Arizona Technology Council. In early 2021 the company expanded its headquarters in the heart of mid-town Tucson, featuring an open floor plan with large windows, standing desks, lab space, and flexible work hours. This collaborative culture emphasizes creativity, teamwork, inclusion, and innovation.

FreeFall Aerospace is an active member of the Arizona Technology Council. The company also participates in educational programs for youth in STEM with the following organizations: SheTech, TenWest, SARSEF, WISE, and the Metropolitan Education Commission.

Name: MIRAD microwave AG
Description:
MIRAD microwave is the trusted supplier of state of the art antenna transmitter, receiver, tracking and filter technology for the satellite communication industry. Our solutions and components are key factors for the success of global communication systems and interplanetary space missions.

For satellite up and downlink signals MIRAD is the trusted provider of passive RF infrastructure hardware and services for ground station antennas of any type and make.

From 1 GHz up to 70 GHz, we deliver a complete range of antenna & waveguide solutions with Swiss precision & quality.
We design and manufacture: 
- Feed systems (single and multi-band)
- Feed networks
- Switching & combining systems
- Standard waveguide component like directional couplers, terminations, 
   filters, diplexers, OMTs, waveguide routing,     air inlets and pressure windows

We also provide:
- Antenna geometries for the main- and sub-reflector for new ground stations 
   including reflector deformation analysis
- Antenna performance simulations (antenna efficiency, radiation pattern, G/T 
   calculations)
- Definition of antenna key parameters as a starting point for the feed system 
   design
- Feasibility assessment of antenna upgrades 
- Beam waveguide calculation

In our in-house anechoic chamber (8m x 6m x 6m), MIRAD tests the antenna characteristics of our own systems and prototypes, as well as third party RF products up to 70 GHz. (contact us to check availability and for a quote). Our measurements are three dimensional and in high resolution. The test procedures are software defined for synchronic VNA and hardware-motion controlling.
All our solutions consistently deliver the best possible RF performance, due to our proven RF engineering skills and in-house availability of adequate simulation and testing infrastructure.

Name: International Telecommunications Satellite Organization
Description: ITSO is an intergovernmental organization with 149-member states, that incorporates the principle set forth in Resolution 1721 (XVI) of the General Assembly of the United Nations, which established that communication by means of satellite should be available to the nations of the world as soon as practicable on a global and non-discriminatory basis. Originally established in 1973 as INTELSAT, the International Telecommunications Satellite Organization was restructured in 2001. The restructuring led to the creation of a private entity, Intelsat S.A. and to the continuation of the intergovernmental organization with a new acronym (ITSO). ITSO´s mission is to monitor the performance of Intelsat’s public service obligations and to safeguard the Parties’ Common Heritage.

Name: ETHER project
Description: ETHER aims to provide a holistic approach for integrated terrestrial-non-terrestrial networks targeting at 100% network coverage, 99.99999% service continuity and 99.99999% reliability, with 3 times higher energy efficiency and 95% Total Cost of Ownership reduction compared to current terrestrial only deployments. To achieve these goals, ETHER develops solutions for a Unified Radio Access Network (RAN) and for the energy-efficient, AI-enabled resource management across the terrestrial, aerial and space domains, while creating the business plans driving future investments in the area. To that end, ETHER introduces and combines a series of key technologies under a unique 3D multi-layered architectural proposition that brings together: i) a UE antenna design and implementation for direct handheld access in the integrated network, ii) a robust unified waveform, iii) energy-efficient seamless horizontal and vertical handover policies, iv) a zero-touch management and network orchestrator to self-adapt to rapidly evolving traffic conditions without human intervention, v) a flexible payload system to enable programmability in the aerial and space layers, vi) joint communication, compute and storage resource allocation solutions targeting at End-to-End network performance optimization leveraging efficient and novel predictive analytics schemes, and vii) energy-efficient semantics-aware information handling techniques combined with edge computing and caching for reduced latency across the distributed 3D compute and storage continuum.

Name: Challenger Communications, L.L.C.
Description:
Challenger Communications has the ability to manufacture a complete line of satellite antenna systems. These antennas include, but are not limited to:

• 3.8m, 4.5m, 6.3m, 7.5m Prime Focus Satellite Dish Antennas 
• 3.8m Offset Tx/Rx Antenna Systems 
• Flyaway and Man pack Quick-Deploy/Mobile Satellite Antennas in Offset Sizes of 1.0m, 1.2m, 1.8m
--------------------------

Challenger Communications, LLC is a leading manufacturer of world class antenna systems and mounts. We offer a range of antennas from 1.0m to 7.8 Meters to fit your needs.  3.8 - 7.5m C/Ku & GOES West antenna solutions. Quick-deploy & mobile satellite antennas. MADE IN USA.

Challenger Communications, LLC was formed by former Patriot Antenna Systems Vice President and Director of Manufacturing, Gene Sorgi, in 2011.

Challenger has new 3.8 Meter & 4.5 Meter Prime Focus aluminum antennas in stock and ready to ship. The tooling used to manufacture these new antennas was previously used to produce some of the best-selling, high-quality antennas known in the satellite communications industry. The new antennas are available in fixed and motorized configurations. The new antennas are simpler to install than previous models, and no crane is required for installation.

3.8m & 4.5m all aluminum reflectors have been proven to deliver a superior performance over fiberglass and sheet molding compound (SMC) and wire mesh satellite antenna reflectors. 

Enhanced Quality
After months of re-tooling, the new Challenger 3.8m & 4.5m antennas have been enhanced to perform with greater accuracy than previous models. 

Improved Packaging
Packaging of both the 3.8m & 4.m Prime Focus Antenna Systems and mounts has been reconfigured to make shipping more efficient. The improved packaging offers greater durability and allows Challenger to fit almost twice as many units into a sea container than current competitors.

Bivy then launched the Bivy Stick, which allows you to turn your cell phone into a satellite communication device. Anywhere you have a view of the sky, you can communicate with the Bivy Stick. Two-way text messaging, SOS, location sharing, detailed weather reports are sent straight to your cell phone from space via the Iridium satellite network. When your adventures take you off the grid, the Bivy Stick keeps you safe, gives you peace of mind and is an essential piece of your adventure kit.

Name: IDRS
Description:
The Inter–Satellite Data Relay System (IDRS) offers the world’s first on-demand, real time connection to any LEO satellite. Developed by communication leaders Addvalue, this revolutionary system leverages the INMARSAT-4 constellation and BGAN ground infrastructure to achieve global coverage and connectivity for LEO satellites, and can support data rates in excess of 200 Kbps, in all orbital inclinations and at altitudes of up to 1000 km.

The result: “Always On” low-latency connectivity, provided in real time. Simplify your end–to–end LEO operations with streamlined, direct routing of client data, automated frequency management, reduced infrastructure overheads, shorter commissioning periods and increased value of time sensitive EO images.

IDRS offers a complete solution, combining full service support for the provision of space–qualified hardware and operational airtime. With the ability to support mission tasking, mission data, satellite telemetry and command operations, the opportunities that IDRS unlocks are significant.
IDRS terminals are already in active operation on a range of LEO constellations. If you’re interested in learning more about what this ground-breaking innovation could represent to your own fleet operations, please don’t hesitate to contact our team.

Our main services include:
● Dedicated & On-Demand ground station receiving services for spacecraft & launch vehicles (S-Band, UHF/VHF, L-/C-/X-Band).
● Lost satellite support: If a customer hasn't established contact or lost contact with their satellite, we offer extensive searches for any weak RF signals, help locate the satellite in orbit and re-establish communications.
● Dedicated tracking support for satellite operators in the weeks after launch. We perform frequent tracking measurements and keep track of our customers satellites in orbit. Significantly reducing operations risks and ensuring stable RF communications.
● Object Identification: We accurately and quickly identify which object the customers satellite is after launch. - Allowing for smooth and fast-paced OPS and improving space-safety for our customer and everyone else in orbit.
● Space Domain Awareness (SDA/SSA): RF sensor-as-a-service, Tracking, Object ID and spectrum monitoring offerings for space situational awareness.
● On-demand ground station receiving services of long-range line-of-sight RF downlinks from aerial vehicles. We can i.e. receive and forward secure live video feeds from fixed wing aircraft/helicopters/UAVs over a distance of hundreds of kilometers. This is often used in Search & Rescue, Disaster response or broadcast events.

Whether you’re planning your next mission or need urgent support, we’re flexible. Get in touch — we’d love to support your operations.

Name: GNSS MAGICA Project
Description:
Under the sponsorship of the European Union Agency for the Space Programme (EUSPA) the MAGICA project will develop the first multi-constellation Galileo antenna with multi-frequency capability specially adapted to automotive applications.

The proposed MAGICA antenna is intended to be going beyond the state of the art since, for the first time, it will provide a cost-effective high precision positioning antenna (providing multi-frequency characteristic and phase stability as the most relevant performance feature) commercially ready to be integrated on a vehicle for Autonomous Driving operation.

The major innovations that will result from the MAGICA project are:

- GNSS/Galileo multi-constellation/multi-frequency cost-effective new radiating element for integration on a vehicle.
- Design & Development of a new RF LNA with proper Noise Figure, linearity, frequency response and group delay between bands to ensure multi-frequency operation and antenna phase stability for centimetre level accuracy of PPP and RTK receivers.
- Different allocations of the antenna inside the vehicle and the effect on the antenna performance with the integration on the vehicle.
- Optimal integration for the automotive applications with testing on real scenarios and with reference RTK and PPP high precision GNSS receivers.
- New laboratory Test Bench with simulated real multi-frequency and multi-constellation GNSS signal to evaluate the whole system accuracy with the new antenna design.
- Integration of all of the antenna elements in a vehicle close to commercialization.

Name: Atharva Satellite Ground Station
Description:
Atharva Satellite Ground Station is one of the major student committees from Atharva College of Engineering which aims to research and develop/fabricate different analog/digital antennas and projects related to communication.
Atharva Satellite Ground Station was established in 2008 as a part of IIT Bombay’s first student Satellite “PRATHAM” under the visionary leadership of Shri. Sunil Rane, Executive President, Atharva Group of Institutes who believes in Research, Technology, and Innovation.
Atharva’s Ground Station innovative students team achieved the milestone in establishing a functional receiving Ground Station for IIT Bombay’s PRATHAM satellite at Atharva College of Engineering. The student satellite, Pratham, was launched by ISRO’s four-stage Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) at the Sriharikota’s Satish Dhawan Space Centre successfully on 26 September 2016 at 9.00 a.m. the Pratham satellites were lifted off. The dream project of IIT-B MISSION “PRATHAM” in association with Atharva College of Engineering’s Ground station team is finally accomplished. Atharva Ground station is one of the receiving stations to receive and process data sent by Pratham Satellite.

Name: 5G-NTN book: Technologies, Standards, and System Design
Description:
5G Non-Terrestrial Networks is the first multi-authored reference dedicated to the integration of non-terrestrial networks (NTN) into the 5G ecosystem. Written by leaders in the development of 3GPP 5G NTN specification, this authoritative resource addresses all key aspects of non-terrestrial component of 5G systems, including standardization, architecture, protocols, and regulatory considerations.

Drawing from their expertise in academic and industrial research and development, the authors introduce fundamental principles of non-terrestrial communications, define the NTN architecture and radio protocol stacks, describe applications to support mobility and radio resource management, and more. The book covers 5G New Radio-based technology for NTN as well as LTE NB-IoT/eMTC, providing a well-rounded understanding of the unique characteristics of 5G-NTN systems. Throughout the text, the authors offer insights on various design approaches, technical choices, and trade-off options.

In addition, the book:
- Addresses the integration of non-terrestrial networks into 5G systems at all levels
- Describes the principles of non-terrestrial systems, including orbital parameters, link budget, propagation, and space/ground segments
- Includes a detailed overview of 5G-NTN system architectures, deployment scenarios, and spectrum aspects
- Covers NB-IoT and eMTC in NTN, NTN use cases, 5G QoS, and New Radio
- Discusses the potential of non-terrestrial components of 5G in the future 6G ecosystem

5G Non-Terrestrial Networks is a must-have for communication engineers, satellite network operators, aerospace and electrical engineers, network engineers, academic researchers and industry professionals involved in 5G Infrastructure development, as well as advanced students taking courses on 5G and satellite communication.

Name: ENAC - SIGNAV
Description:
The SIGnal processing and NAVigation (SIGNAV) is a research group within the ENAC laboratory. SIGNAV research activities tackle the development and analysis of signal processing techniques and algorithms, with a primary focus on navigation and positioning topics in which Global Navigation Satellite Systems play a key role, and a secondary focus on communication signals topics. A key and historical application of the SIGNAV group is the aeronautical telecommunications systems as defined by ICAO.  

We participate in PhD thesis supervision, in national or international research projects, and in academic teaching of ENAC engineers (IENAC) and of MSc in Aerospace Systems - Navigation and Telecommunications (AS-NAT) students.

Name: TelemaTek
Description:
TelemaTek olarak, Nesnelerin İnterneti (IoT) alanında uçtan uca çözümler geliştiren bir teknoloji firmasıyız. Modern donanım ve yazılım sistemleriyle verileri, cihazları ve insanları akıllı bir ekosistemde bir araya getiriyoruz.


Sadece donanım üretmekle kalmıyor, aynı zamanda bu sistemlerin verimli çalışmasını sağlayan yazılımları da geliştiriyoruz.


TelemaTek’in cihazları temeli, yüksek performanslı bir işlemci ve korumalı bellek modüllerinden oluşur. Bu sayede en zorlu çalışma koşullarında bile istikrarlı bir performans sergiler. Dayanıklı gövdesi ve özenle tasarlanmış yapısı, dış etkenlere ve sıcaklık dalgalanmalarına karşı koruma sağlar.

Farklı türdeki cihazları akıllı bir ağda birleştirerek, bu cihazların veri toplamasını, işlemesini ve birbirleriyle güvenli şekilde iletişim kurmasını sağlıyoruz. Bu sayede işletmelerin verimliliği, güvenliği ve kontrol kabiliyeti önemli ölçüde artıyor.


Her ölçekteki işletmenin dijital dönüşümünü hızlandırmak, varlık yönetimini akıllı sistemlerle kolaylaştırmak ve müşterilerimize değer katan teknolojiler sunmaya devam etmektir.

Name: UC3-Base
Description: UC3-Base es una asociación de radioaficionados apasionados por la tecnología, las comunicaciones y la ciencia. Fundada en el entorno académico de la Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, nos dedicamos a la experimentación y la innovación en el campo de las comunicaciones por radio y satélite. Nuestro equipo está compuesto por estudiantes, profesores y entusiastas de diversas áreas, todos unidos por el interés común de expandir las fronteras del conocimiento en el ámbito de las telecomunicaciones. A lo largo de los años, hemos trabajado en múltiples proyectos de comunicaciones satelitales, radiofrecuencia y antenas innovadoras. Además, fomentamos el desarrollo personal y profesional de nuestros miembros a través de talleres, conferencias y eventos, brindando una plataforma para aprender y compartir experiencias.

Name: FOCAL MSCA Project
Description:
FOCAL (Free-space Optical Communications
for Aerial-satellite networks) is a 4 years project in R&I funded by the European Union. In the frame of Horizon Europe, FOCAL will establish a comprehensive and interdisciplinary Doctoral network on the development of advanced optical space and aerial technologies, offering ubiquitous connectivity, resilience, and security for the future wireless network infrastructures.

Name: SIGMA
Description:
Satellite-enabled Interoperable system ensuring GOVSATCOM services’ reliability, optimal traffic Management, security and long-term Availability for EU and national public authorities.

In recent years, Satellite Communications have gained momentum, ensuring global connectivity and reliable communication for defense, security, humanitarian aid, and emergency response. The EU GOVSATCOM initiative aims to create a new SatCom solution for EU and national entities. The SIGMA project proposes a technical solution that integrates capacities from different satellite systems, including SatCom-On-The-Move operating in X-band, military Ka-band, and civil Ka-band, along with 5G user equipment. Traffic management and end-to-end security are key components, and demonstrations will be conducted in real user environments across three EU countries.

This project, falls within the  EUSPA - EU Agency for the Space Programme, and has received funding EUROPE HORIZON.  #EUSpace

Name: Hydramo
Description:
Hydramo is a highly innovative startup focused on Earth Observation and mission-critical operations. We develop next-generation solutions that integrate in-situ sensor data with satellite observations to enable real-time environmental awareness. Our EO2GO App functions as a satellite early-warning platform, delivering Earth Observation insights directly to citizens, emergency responders, and field teams — even when conventional communication networks fail.

Our work aligns with Pillars 2, 3, and 4 of the Early Warnings for All (EW4All) initiative, combining technological innovation with practical deployment. We bridge the gap between space-based data, local sensing, and actionable field-level information. Hydramo delivers capabilities that support climate adaptation, flood resilience, disaster preparedness, and operational decision-making.

We are committed to advancing technologies that directly serve people and planet, contributing to a sustainable and secure future for communities worldwide.
